<div align="center">

# AHOY

### Adversarial Harness for Objective Yielding

**Code generated by AI coding agents, evaluated by *independent* external models.**

[![Python 3.12+](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.12%2B-blue?logo=python&logoColor=white)](#requirements)
[![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Claude_Code-plugin-blueviolet?logo=anthropic&logoColor=white)](#installation)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green)](#license)

</div>

---

## Problem

AI coding agents evaluate their own code. The same model that writes it judges it вЂ” same context, same biases, same blind spots.

<p align="center">
  <img src="docs/assets/problem-solution.svg" alt="Problem vs Solution" width="720"/>
</p>

## Solution

AHOY **physically separates** the Generator from the Evaluator.

- The Generator (Claude) writes code. **External models** (Codex, Gemini, Claude CLI) evaluate it in a **separate process**.
- Claude can only **read** external verdicts вЂ” it **cannot modify** them.
- A hook system enforces rules at the **shell level** вЂ” prompt bypass is impossible.

---

## Installation

AHOY is a **Claude Code plugin**. Install it in 2 steps:

```bash
# 1. Add the marketplace & install
/plugin marketplace add mullung2/ahoy
/plugin install ahoy

# 2. Verify your environment
/ahoy:ahoy-setup
```

> `/ahoy:ahoy-setup` checks Python, uv, and external model CLIs вЂ” and tells you exactly what to fix if anything is missing.

### Requirements

| Tool | Version | Purpose |
|------|---------|---------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | latest | Harness runtime |
| [Codex CLI](https://github.com/openai/codex) | latest | External evaluator #1 |
| [Gemini CLI](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli) or [Claude CLI](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-cli) | latest | External evaluator #2 |
| Python | 3.12+ | eval_dispatch.py runtime |

> At least 2 external model CLIs must be installed for consensus.

<details>
<summary>Alternative: local development mode</summary>

```bash
claude --plugin-dir /path/to/ahoy
```
</details>

### Usage

```bash
# Start a new harness
/ahoy <project request>

# Resume an interrupted harness
/ahoy

# Individual steps (usually called automatically by the Orchestrator)
/ahoy:ahoy-plan <request>
/ahoy:ahoy-gen
/ahoy:ahoy-eval
```

---

## Architecture

<p align="center">
  <img src="docs/assets/architecture.svg" alt="AHOY Architecture" width="780"/>
</p>

### Sprint State Machine

<p align="center">
  <img src="docs/assets/state-machine.svg" alt="Sprint State Machine" width="720"/>
</p>

| State | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `planned` | Sprint contract created, awaiting user confirmation |
| `contracted` | User approved, ready for Generator |
| `generated` | Code implemented, awaiting external evaluation |
| `passed` | External consensus = pass |
| `failed` | External consensus = fail, rework needed |
| `rework` | Generator fixing issues found by external models |
| `skipped` | User chose to skip this sprint |

---

## How It Works

### 1. Plan

The Planner analyzes the user request and decomposes it into **sprint contracts**.

```
User: "Build JWT auth + user CRUD API"
                    в”‚
                    в–ј
  sprint-001: JWT Authentication (3 ACs)
  sprint-002: User CRUD (4 ACs)
  sprint-003: Permission Management (3 ACs)
```

Each charter contains **verifiable acceptance criteria (ACs)**:
- **Good**: `POST /api/users в†’ returns 201, creates user record in DB`
- **Bad**: `Implement user management`

### 2. Generate

The Generator (Claude) implements code and tests according to the contract.

- Only files listed in contract.md's scope
- Executable tests mapped to each AC
- **No self-evaluation** вЂ” words like "pass", "satisfied", "complete" are prohibited in reports

### 3. Evaluate

**External models evaluate in a separate process** вЂ” no shared context with the Generator.

```bash
python ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/eval_dispatch.py \
  .claude/harness/sprints/sprint-001 \
  --models codex,claude \
  --project-root .
```

```json
{
  "verdict": "pass",
  "model_verdicts": {"codex": "pass", "claude": "pass"},
  "issues": [],
  "passed_criteria": ["AC-001", "AC-002", "AC-003"]
}
```

**Consensus Rule**: any fail в†’ final fail. Any `partial_pass` в†’ final `partial_pass`. Less than 2 valid models в†’ error.

`issues.json` records both:
- `verdict` вЂ” raw external-model consensus (`pass`, `partial_pass`, `fail`, `error`)
- `status_action` вЂ” harness action (`passed`, `failed`, `error`)

`partial_pass` with only minor issues maps to `status_action: "passed"`. `partial_pass` with any blocker/major issue maps to `status_action: "failed"`.

Models are called **in parallel** via `ThreadPoolExecutor`.

### 4. Rework or Advance

- **pass** в†’ advance to next sprint
- **fail** в†’ rework based on issues found by external models (max 3 attempts)
- **3 failures** в†’ escalate to user for decision

---

## Skills

AHOY uses Claude Code **skills** (not legacy commands) for full plugin compatibility.

| Skill | Role | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| **`/ahoy`** | **Orchestrator** | **Full lifecycle management. The only skill you need** |
| `/ahoy:ahoy-setup` | **Setup** | **Verify prerequisites and diagnose environment** |
| `/ahoy:ahoy-plan` | Planner | Request в†’ spec.md + sprint contracts |
| `/ahoy:ahoy-gen` | Generator | Implements code/tests per contract.md (Claude) |
| `/ahoy:ahoy-eval` | Evaluator | Dispatches external model evaluation + records results |

> Sub-skills (`ahoy-plan`, `ahoy-gen`, `ahoy-eval`) have `disable-model-invocation: true` вЂ” they are only invoked by the orchestrator, not automatically by Claude.

---

## Safety Mechanisms

AHOY's safety works at the **shell level**, not the prompt level.
Even if Claude ignores the prompt, hooks physically block violations.

### Layer 1 вЂ” File Ownership Enforcement

```
issues.json в†’ only eval_dispatch.py (subprocess) can write
              Claude's Write/Edit в†’ hook unconditionally blocks (exit 1)
```

### Layer 2 вЂ” State Transition Guards

```
generated в†’ passed transition requires:
  вњ“ issues.json exists?
  вњ“ integrity check passed?
  вњ“ valid external models в‰Ґ 2?
  вњ“ verdict в†” status consistent?

  Any failure в†’ transition blocked + automatic rollback
```

### Layer 3 вЂ” Pre/Post Hook Matrix

| Timing | Matcher | Script | Blocks When |
|--------|---------|--------|-------------|
| Pre | `Write\|Edit(*harness_state*)` | `pre-state-write` | Transition from generated without evaluation |
| Post | `Write\|Edit(*harness_state*)` | `post-state-write` | passed в†” verdict mismatch в†’ **auto rollback** |
| Pre | `Write\|Edit(*issues.json*)` | `guard-eval-files` | Claude writing issues.json directly (always) |
| Pre | `Bash\|Agent(*ahoy-gen*)` | `pre-gen` | Generator without contract.md |
| Post | `Bash(*eval_dispatch*)` | `post-eval` | verdict error/unknown, valid models < 2 |
| Pre | `Bash(git commit*)` | `pre-commit` | Test failure |
| Pre | `Bash(git push*)` | `pre-push` | Test failure or state inconsistency |

Hook scripts use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` paths вЂ” they work regardless of where the plugin is installed.

### Layer 4 вЂ” Generator Opinion Filtering

Generator reports are **sanitized** before being sent to external models.
Subjective judgments ("satisfied", "pass", "complete") are stripped.
Only factual information remains: file lists, test results, line counts.

---

## Plugin Structure

```
ahoy/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .claude-plugin/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ plugin.json                  # Plugin manifest
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ marketplace.json             # Marketplace registry
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .mcp.json                        # MCP server config (Codex)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ hooks/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ hooks.json                   # 7 enforcement hooks
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ skills/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ahoy/SKILL.md               # Orchestrator
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ahoy-setup/SKILL.md         # Setup & diagnostics
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ahoy-plan/SKILL.md          # Planner
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ahoy-gen/SKILL.md           # Generator
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ahoy-eval/SKILL.md          # Evaluator
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ scripts/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ eval_dispatch.py             # External model eval dispatcher (parallel)
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ validate_harness.py          # State transition validator + auto rollback
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ templates/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ spec_template.md             # Project spec template
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ sprint_contract_template.md  # Sprint contract template
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ eval_report_template.md      # Evaluation report template
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ handoff_template.md          # Context handoff template
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ docs/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ README.ko.md                 # Korean README
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ assets/                      # SVG diagrams
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ CLAUDE.md                        # Claude Code context
в””в”Ђв”Ђ README.md                        # This file
```

### Workspace (generated per project)

```
.claude/harness/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ harness_state.json         # Master state
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ spec.md                    # Planner output
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ sprints/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ sprint-NNN/
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ contract.md        # Sprint contract
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ gen_report.md      # Generator report (facts only)
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ eval_report.md     # Evaluator report
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ issues.json        # External model verdict (Claude write-blocked)
в””в”Ђв”Ђ handoffs/
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ handoff-NNN.md         # Context reset handoff
```

---

## Design Principles

| # | Principle | Enforcement |
|---|-----------|-------------|
| 1 | **Generator never evaluates** | Hooks physically block self-evaluation paths |
| 2 | **Contract is truth** | Generator and Evaluator reference the same contract.md |
| 3 | **File-based communication** | State shared via JSON/MD files, no memory dependency |
| 4 | **Consensus required** | Single-model pass prevented, minimum 2 valid models |
| 5 | **Context reset** | Handoff document every 3 sprints for fresh sessions |

---

## Supported External Models

| Model | CLI | Command | Status |
|-------|-----|---------|--------|
| OpenAI Codex | `codex` | `codex exec --yolo --ephemeral` | Supported |
| Google Gemini | `gemini` | `gemini -p` | Supported |
| Anthropic Claude | `claude` | `claude -p` (separate process) | Supported |

> Add new models by extending `call_model()` in `eval_dispatch.py`.

## Roadmap

- [ ] Web UI dashboard вЂ” real-time sprint progress monitoring
- [ ] Evaluator plugin system вЂ” direct API calls alongside CLI
- [ ] Evaluation history analytics вЂ” model agreement rates, issue pattern trends
- [ ] CI/CD integration вЂ” run AHOY loops in GitHub Actions
- [ ] Multi-agent Generator вЂ” multiple models implement, best output selected
